subject: [RFC PATCH 09/40] mm: page_alloc: support superpageblock resize for memory hotplug

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch adds support for resizing superpageblocks when memory is hotplugged. It introduces a new function `resize_zone_superpageblocks` that allocates a new superpageblock array covering the full zone span, copies existing superpageblocks, and initializes new ones for the added range. This ensures that pages in newly hotplugged regions are steered by superpageblocks, preventing them from bypassing anti-fragmentation steering entirely. The patch also adds a boolean flag `spb_kvmalloced` to track whether superpageblocks were allocated using kvmalloc (hotplug).
